evidence: interruptedGenerationContextIds is a Set with exactly four operation sites in voice-agent-session.ts: add at :925 (handleInterruptDetected -> this.interruptedGenerationContextIds.add(pkt.contextId)), has() gates at :724 (handleLlmDelta -> 'llm.delta_ignored_after_interrupt' return), :757 (handleLlmDone -> deletes ttsTextBuffer + 'llm.done_ignored_after_interrupt' return), :860 (handleTtsAudio -> 'tts.audio_ignored_after_interrupt' return), plus a watchdog read at :298. The ONLY removal is this.interruptedGenerationContextIds.clear() at :389, inside closeOnce(). grep confirms NO .delete() exists anywhere; handleTurnComplete (:676) and handleVadSpeechStarted (:620) do not touch the Set. So a contextId, once interrupted, is gated for the life of the session UNLESS a fresh contextId is minted for the next turn. The contextId is NOT minted per-turn on the telephony transports: in twilio.ts state.contextId is set once at the start event (:294) to `twilio-${callSid}` (defaultTwilioContextId :577) and reused for every user.audio_received frame (:604, contextId: state.contextId) across all turns of the call; telnyx.ts is identical (:312 set once to `telnyx-${callControlId}`, :605 reused); smartpbx.ts identical (:259 set once to `smartpbx-${callId}`, :278 reused). On the browser websocket path (index.ts) contextId is client-supplied with currentContextId fallback (:650 nextContextId = binaryAudio.contextId ?? currentContextId, :693 message.contextId ?? currentContextId) and the server performs NO per-turn rotation on eos.turn_complete (:444-447 only forwards turn_complete to the client). Therefore on every telephony call, and on any browser client that does not rotate contextId per turn, turn N+1 reuses the same contextId; after a single barge-in on turn N, all of turn N+1's LLM deltas (:724), llm.done (:757) and TTS audio (:860) are silently dropped for the remainder of the call. The bug applies to the entire telephony fleet (callId-stable), not a narrow edge case, so critical severity holds.
real_fix: Remove the contextId from interruptedGenerationContextIds when a NEW turn begins on that contextId, so a stable per-call contextId is re-armed each turn. Concrete options, in order of robustness: (1) Best fix for the stable-contextId reality: in handleTurnComplete (packages/voice/src/voice-agent-session.ts:676) and/or handleVadSpeechStarted (:620) call this.interruptedGenerationContextIds.delete(pkt.contextId) — i.e. clear the interrupt flag at the start of the user's next turn, mirroring how :927-928 already delete firstLlmDeltaReceived/ttsTextBuffers per context. (2) Alternatively scope generation identity to a per-turn nonce instead of the call-stable contextId so the has() check is naturally turn-scoped. Note kimi's suggested 'clear on eos.turn_complete or tts.end' must NOT be done on tts.end of the interrupted turn — that would race with the late packets it is meant to drop; the safe clear point is the START of the next user turn (turn_complete / vad.speech_started of N+1), not the end of turn N. Recommend (1) gated on the next turn's speech start. Add a regression test in voice-agent-session.test.ts: same contextId, interrupt turn N, then drive turn N+1 deltas/audio and assert they are emitted (not '*_ignored_after_interrupt').
files: packages/voice/src/voice-agent-session.ts:210,298,389,724,757,860,925 (Set never per-turn cleared); packages/voice-server-websocket/src/twilio.ts:294,577,604 (call-stable contextId); packages/voice-server-websocket/src/telnyx.ts:312,605,684 (call-stable contextId); packages/voice-server-websocket/src/smartpbx.ts:259,278,506 (call-stable contextId); packages/voice-server-websocket/src/index.ts:444-447,650,693 (no server-side per-turn rotation). Fix site: voice-agent-session.ts handleTurnComplete (~:676) and/or handleVadSpeechStarted (~:620).
